info.name: Azure DevOps Work Items API
info.description:
REST API for managing work items, work item types, fields, queries, attachments, and comments in Azure Boards. Enables programmatic access to agile planning and tracking resources across sprints, backlogs, and boards.


Contact Support:
 Name: Microsoft Azure DevOps

item.name: Azure DevOps Create a work item

query.description: (Required) Azure DevOps REST API version. Use 7.1 for the latest stable version.
Validate the fields without saving the work item
Do not enforce the work item type rules on this update
Do not fire any notifications for this change

variable.description: (Required) Work item type (e.g., Bug, Task, User Story)

request.description: Creates a new work item of the specified type. The request body must be a JSON Patch document describing the fields to set on the new work item. Common types include Bug, Task, User Story, Feature, and Epic.

response.name: Work item created successfully

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

item.name: Azure DevOps Get work item comments

query.description: (Required) Azure DevOps REST API version. Use 7.1 for the latest stable version.
Maximum number of comments to return
Continuation token for paging

variable.description: (Required) Numeric ID of the work item

request.description: Returns all comments for a specific work item, ordered by creation date. Comments are user-authored notes attached to a work item and are separate from the work item's history.

response.name: Comments returned successfully

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

item.name: Azure DevOps Add a comment to a work item

query.description: (Required) Azure DevOps REST API version. Use 7.1 for the latest stable version.

variable.description: (Required) Numeric ID of the work item

request.description: Adds a new comment to a work item. The comment body supports HTML markup. The new comment is appended to the existing list of comments.

response.name: Comment added successfully

item.name: Azure DevOps Update a work item

query.description: (Required) Azure DevOps REST API version. Use 7.1 for the latest stable version.
Validate fields without saving the work item
Do not enforce work item type rules on this update
Do not fire any notifications for this change

variable.description: (Required) Numeric ID of the work item to update

request.description: Updates an existing work item by applying a JSON Patch document. Supports updating fields, adding relations, and modifying links. Use the add, replace, remove, and test operations to make changes.

response.name: Work item updated successfully

item.name: Azure DevOps Delete a work item

query.description: (Required) Azure DevOps REST API version. Use 7.1 for the latest stable version.
Permanently delete the work item instead of moving to recycle bin

variable.description: (Required) Numeric ID of the work item to delete

request.description: Moves a work item to the recycle bin. The work item can be recovered from the recycle bin or permanently deleted. Deletion requires appropriate permissions.

response.name: Work item deleted successfully

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

item.name: Azure DevOps List work items by IDs

query.description: (Required) Azure DevOps REST API version. Use 7.1 for the latest stable version.
(Required) Comma-separated list of work item IDs (up to 200)
Comma-separated list of fields to return (e.g., System.Title,System.State)
Work items as of a specific date and time (ISO 8601 format)
Expand relations, links, or fields in the response

request.description: Returns a list of work items by their IDs. You must provide a comma-separated list of work item IDs. Optionally filter by specific fields, as-of date, or expand relations and links.

response.name: List of work items returned successfully

item.name: Azure DevOps Execute a WIQL query

query.description: (Required) Azure DevOps REST API version. Use 7.1 for the latest stable version.
Maximum number of results to return
Whether or not to use time precision

request.description: Executes a Work Item Query Language (WIQL) query and returns a list of work item references that match the query criteria. WIQL is a SQL-like language specific to Azure DevOps work item querying.

response.name: Query results returned successfully

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

item.name: Azure DevOps Get a work item type

query.description: (Required) Azure DevOps REST API version. Use 7.1 for the latest stable version.

variable.description: (Required) Name of the work item type

request.description: Returns detailed information about a specific work item type, including its fields, transitions, states, and rules. The type name is case-sensitive and must match exactly (e.g., 'Bug', 'Task', 'User Story').

response.name: Work item type returned successfully

item.name: Azure DevOps List work item types

query.description: (Required) Azure DevOps REST API version. Use 7.1 for the latest stable version.

request.description: Returns a list of all work item types defined in the project. Work item types include Bug, Task, User Story, Feature, Epic, and any custom types defined in the process template.

response.name: List of work item types returned successfully

request.description: Returns a list of all work item fields available in the project. Includes both system fields (System.*) and custom fields, with their types, reference names, and whether they are read-only.
